description: Anonymized participant ID
Participant age in years
Participant gender (male/female)
Years of education
Self-reported vision quality (1=excellent 20/20 or better, 7=poor 20/50)
University at which the participant was tested (msu/washu/suny/omaha)
Operation span score: sum of letters recalled in correct order (0–75)
Antisaccade task accuracy (proportion correct)
Stroop interference RT: mean RT in incongruent minus congruent condition (ms)
Stroop interference error rate: error rate in incongruent minus congruent condition
Attentional control composite score (PCA of ospan / saccade / stroop)
Woodcock-Johnson III passage comprehension score
Woodcock-Johnson III synonym test score
Woodcock-Johnson III antonym test score
Woodcock-Johnson III analogy test score
Morningness-Eveningness Questionnaire (MEQ) score (16–86; higher = more morning-type)
Session number (1 or 2)
Trial order index within participant (1-indexed, sorted by Session, Block, Trial for LDT; Session, Trial for naming)
Prime word presented before the target (lowercase)
Type of prime: first_associate (most common association in Nelson et al. 1999 norms) or other_associate (2nd-Nth most common association to a different cue word)
Stimulus onset asynchrony in ms: 200 (short, associated with automatic priming) or 1200 (long, associated with intentional priming)
Semantic relatedness of the prime-target pair: related or unrelated (unrelated pairs formed by random re-pairing within the same prime-type set)
Target word or nonword presented to the participant
Whether the target is a real word ('word') or a pronounceable nonword ('nonword'). Half of word targets were replaced by nonwords per participant.
Participant response: 'word' or 'nonword' for LDT (exp1, inferred from target.ACC and lexicality); 'correct', 'unsure', 'mispronunciation', or 'extraneous' for speeded naming (exp2)
Response time in milliseconds. For naming (exp2), set to NaN if a microphone error was detected.
Response accuracy (1.0 = correct, 0.0 = incorrect)
